content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the right ahead with the dashed center line visible curving right, and the car appears to be on the left side of the track navigating this right-hand bend.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 104, target = (104 + 5) % 120 = 109
- Waypoint 109: x=1.3394, y=0.9674
- Car: x=1.3036, y=1.3281
- Bearing = atan2(0.9674 - 1.3281, 1.3394 - 1.3036) = atan2(-0.3607, 0.0358) ≈ -84.3°
- Current heading: -42.02°
- Delta = -84.3° - (-42.02°) = -42.3° → right turn needed

Delta ≈ -42° confirms significant right turn needed as track curves south toward waypoints 109-115.

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves sharply right ahead (delta ≈ -42°), car is left of center heading south-southwest, continuing right steering to follow the track curving south toward waypoints 109-115.",

    "Track loops sharply south around waypoints 103-115 then curves back northeast",
    "Waypoints 108-115 trend south/southeast — car must steer right significantly here",
    "Car heading -42° needs to decrease further (more negative) to follow track south",
    "Positive delta = steer left, negative delta = steer right",
    "At delta -40 to -45°, use steering_angle=-20 at speed 1.0",
    "Car is left of center — track curving right confirms right steering needed",
    "After waypoint 115, track begins heading northeast — watch for left turn",
    "Avoid oversteer oscillation on straights; use smallest valid angle when delta < 10°",
    "Previous -20° steering at delta -40° was correct, maintaining same action",
    "Track width ~0.76m, distance from center 0.13m — still within safe bounds"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [1.160, 1.206, 1.304],
    "y": [1.420, 1.394, 1.328],
    "yaw": [-50.5, -47.2, -42.0]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": -42.3,
  "speed": 1.0,
  "steering_angle": -20.0
}
```
